title: Pod Security, Security Contexts, Images, Admission, Secrets, and Multi-Tenancy · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Image Supply Chain Admission
summary: A hostile document workload requests privileged host access, mutable images, broad credentials, unrestricted egress, and co-location with sensitive tenants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s image supply chain admission increment in restricted workload and admission policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Require immutable images, provenance, signatures, vulnerability policy, registry allowlists, architecture, pull credentials, and admission decisions with exceptions.
Set non-root identity, groups, filesystem ownership, read-only root, capabilities, privilege escalation, seccomp, host namespaces, devices, and writable mounts from threat evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Require immutable images, provenance, signatures, vulnerability policy, registry allowlists, architecture, pull credentials, and admission decisions with exceptions. A defensible solution applies K8s Image Supply Chain Admission, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to restricted workload and admission policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-security-admission-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-security-admission-4
    artifact: restricted-workload-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s workload security admission 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      pod-security: restricted-v1.36
      runAsNonRoot: true
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities-drop: ALL
      seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
      image: digest-and-signature-required
      admission: fail-closed-with-breakglass
    verify: kubectl auth can-i create pods -n intake && kubectl apply --server-side --dry-run=server -f restricted.yaml
    failure: Reject removed PodSecurityPolicy, namespace-only multi-tenancy, mutable images, privileged host access, broad secrets, or unavailable admission without a plan.
    repair: Enforce versioned Pod Security, minimize container and API authority, isolate network and storage, verify supply chain, and test break-glass recovery.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: imagePullPolicy Always makes a mutable tag immutable and verifies its publisher. This fails the K8s Image Supply Chain Admission contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
